claim: Salt lamps, because they emit negatively charged ions, impart myriad health benefits including reduced anxiety, improved sleep, increased energy, and protection from an “electric smog.”

result.claim: Salt lamps, because they emit negatively charged ions, impart myriad health benefits including reduced anxiety, improved sleep, increased energy, and protection from an “electric smog.”
result.reasoning: The searches conducted did not yield any direct evidence to support the claim that salt lamps emit negatively charged ions or that they impart health benefits such as reduced anxiety, improved sleep, increased energy, or protection from "electric smog." While some propositions and chunks discussed the effects of "negative air ions (NAI)" on physiological responses in rats (Paper ID: 46), these findings do not link NAI production to salt lamps. There is no information in the knowledge base that connects salt lamps to the emission of negative ions or to any of the claimed health benefits.

evidence.text: Life was positively associated with sleep duration for durations up to 9 hours.
